title: Symphyotrichum parviflorum
intro: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, also known as small white aster or small white Michaelmas daisy, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it beneficial for soothing digestive issues, reducing pain, and aiding in wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Symphyotrichum parviflorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum parviflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is commonly known as the small white aster or small white panicle aster. This plant is a member of the genus Symphyotrichum, which is a group of flowering plants that were formerly classified as part of the genus Aster. The Asteraceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes daisies, sunflowers, and many other species. Symphyotrichum parviflorum is native to eastern North America.
uses: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, also known as small white aster, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and gout. Its roots are used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and promote wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Symphyotrichum parviflorum and to confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.
benefits: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, also known as small white aster or smallflower white aster, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. properties: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, also known as small white aster or small white Michaelmas daisy, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential therapeutic properties. constituents: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, a plant species commonly known as small white aster, contains various active constituents. These include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins. The flavonoids present in the plant, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The triterpenoid saponins present in the plant are reported to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.

preparations: Symphyotrichum parviflorum, also known as small white panicle goldenrod, has been used in herbal medicine for various purposes.
